message:
fix(storage): prevent coalescer depth counter underflow with saturating CAS decrement

CommitCoalescer depth tracking:
- Add rq.depth.fetch_add(1) in the enqueue path so that spilled
  requests are counted in the depth counter. Previously, spill_to_repo
  incremented pending_requests but not depth, causing depth to drift
  below the true queue size and potentially underflow to u64::MAX when
  drained.
- Extract coalescer_depth_decrement() helper that uses fetch_update
  with saturating_sub to safely decrement depth without underflow.
  Replace the two bare fetch_sub calls (in self_process_repo's batch
  drain and coalescer_drain_repo_spill) with this safe decrement.

New test — spill_depth_roundtrip_tracks_spilled_requests_without_underflow:
- Spills two requests, asserts depth==2, drains the spill buffer,
  asserts depth==0, restores the spilled work, and asserts depth==2
  with pending_requests==2 in the spill state.

Formatting:
- Line-length compliance for long expressions in shutdown idle check,
  archive frontmatter matching, and last_serviced_us store.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
